page: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.24xlarge is a high-performance computing server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs, utilizing AMD EPYC 9R14 processors running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, but it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in multi-core CPU operations and memory bandwidth. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and software compilation with Clang. Large-block memory bandwidth reads and writes also rank in the top tier, supported by DDR5 memory speeds. Single-core performance, geekbench multi-core scores, and PassMark CPU physics tests are strong, placing in the top 25%. For database operations, Redis SET operations reach top-tier throughput, while PassMark database operations rank as strong.

The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, which represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring direct-attached NVMe drives or hardware-accelerated graphics processing. However, the high memory-to-core ratio of 16 GB per core and the 150 Gbps network baseline make it highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive, network-bound, and parallel computing tasks. The hardware profile is optimized for high-performance computing (HPC), large-scale software compilation, molecular modeling, physics simulations, database operations, and medium-to-large scale LLM prompt processing and text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.24xlarge is a high-performance computing server featuring 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9R14 processors running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding 16.0 GB of RAM per core. While the server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and large-block memory bandwidth. It also demonstrates strong single-core and database performance. This configuration is highly suited for high-performance computing, parallel processing, software compilation, and memory-intensive database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.24xlarge is a high-performance computing server featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth for parallel workloads.

tagline: A dedicated 48-core high-performance computing instance with 768 GB of DDR5 memory and 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated physical AMD EPYC cores running at 3.7 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory (16.0 GB per core)
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and software compilation performance
Optimized for high-performance computing, parallel processing, and database workloads
